studio-charcoal.$description: Studio Charcoal — Page canvas, primary background — a near-black with a green undertone that reads as neutral but feels warmer than pure black

gallery-black.$description: Gallery Black — Feature bands, specimen backgrounds, header bar — pure black for maximum type contrast

charcoal-surface.$description: Charcoal Surface — Elevated surface, input fields, secondary panel backgrounds — one step lighter than the canvas

slate-mist.$description: Slate Mist — Muted accent band, tertiary surface — desaturated blue-gray used as an alternate section background

graphite.$description: Graphite — Mid-tone borders, muted body text on light sections, card outlines

steel.$description: Steel — Secondary borders, subdued link text on dark backgrounds

fog.$description: Fog — Tertiary borders, inactive UI elements, secondary text

ash.$description: Ash — Medium-contrast borders, control outlines, and structural separators. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

marble.$description: Marble — Light-mode page canvas, pale gray that photographs cleanly for specimen presentations

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text on dark backgrounds, type specimen letterforms, button labels, high-contrast surfaces

flare-orange.$description: Flare Orange — Label tags, collection names, editorial annotations — a saturated vermilion that reads as the foundry's signature mark on light backgrounds

signal-red.$description: Signal Red — Hot accent on dark surfaces, inline highlights, the reddest red in the palette for maximum voltage

electric-blue.$description: Electric Blue — Interactive highlights, active states, link emphasis — a bright cyan-blue that pops against black

mint-pulse.$description: Mint Pulse — Decorative text accent, special-occasion highlights — a pale mint used sparingly for emphasis

canary.$description: Canary — Rare chromatic accent, used for the highest-attention text moment on dark backgrounds

blush.$description: Blush — Soft warm accent, subtle text tint — a cream-pink that warms dark sections without competing with type

soehne.$description: SOEHNE — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

shne.$description: Primary UI typeface — used for navigation, body text, buttons, form labels, and all functional interface text. Tight line-heights (0.98–1.20) at display sizes; generous (1.50) for body. Two weights only: regular for content, bold for emphasis. Tabular numerals via tnum for price alignment; ordinals via ordn.

shne-ikon.$description: Iconographic variant of Söhne for interface glyphs, special characters, and numeric UI elements. Same voice as Söhne but with alternate character forms via calt and tabular figures via tnum.

soehneikon.$description: SOEHNE_IKON — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

gallery-black.$description: Surface level 1: Feature band, specimen background

studio-charcoal.$description: Surface level 2: Primary page canvas, page background

charcoal-surface.$description: Surface level 3: Elevated panel, input field
